rationale: Okada (2005) is cited in the main theory/contribution sections when the authors position their work within the purchase-justification literature, rather than only as generic background. The citing paper's central mechanism is entitlement-based justification for socially costly choices, so this citation helps ground core conceptual framing. The appendix also states that purchase-justification items were adapted from Okada (among other sources), indicating concrete measurement use.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Okada (2005) as part of the conceptual foundation for its justification-based account of socially costly purchasing. It also borrows from Okada to operationalize purchase-justification measures in its studies.
